one_time_description: Debt forgiveness of First Nation Negotiation Support Agreements by Minister of Crown-Indigenous Relations; also BC Motor Fuel Tax rebate of $259,830 and extinguished replacement reserve of $88,563
economic_notes: Band operates Nautley Store (retail). Six new homes contracted post year-end for $1,529,155 funded by BMO line of credit. COVID-19 identified as subsequent event. On-reserve housing loan program of $2,000,000 and Treasury Risk Management Facility of $1,440,000. GBEs include Chundo Yus Forestry Ltd, Chundo Yus Forestry LP, Chundo Yus Real Estate Ltd, Chundo Yus Real Estate LP, and Nadleh LP.
notes: Fiscal year is 2019/2020 ended March 31, 2020. Auditor firm name not extractable from provided text. GBEs (5 entities) accounted for under modified equity method. One-time items include: debt forgiveness $1,407,264 (federal negotiation loans forgiven), BC Motor Fuel Tax rebate $259,830, extinguished replacement reserve $88,563. Net debt position of -$8,011,634. Cost of goods sold $1,336,864 deducted before gross profit. Current liabilities calculated as bank indebtedness $1,057,863 + accounts payable $343,679 + due to government $34,787 = $1,436,329 (excluding deferred revenue and long-term debt). Schedule of remuneration page references exist but data not provided in text extract.
